elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

This figure shows a wind speed map of the United States displaying basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures. The map contains contour lines indicating 3-second gust wind speeds in mph at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C, with values in parentheses showing equivalent speeds in m/s.

[Structured: {"type":"map","geography":"Continental United States with state boundaries and coastal areas","parameter":"3-second gust wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings","contour_values":["100(45)","105(47)","110(49)","115(51)","117(52)","120(54)","130(58)","140(63)","150(67)","160(72)","170(76)","175(78)"],"measurement_details":"Wind speeds in mph at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C, with m/s values in parentheses"}]
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text: 1. Values are 3 s gust wind speeds in mi/h (m/s) at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C.
2. Linear interpolation is permitted between contours. Point values are provided to aid with interpolation.
3. Islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour shall use the last wind speed contour.
4. Location-specific basic wind speeds shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
